skill.name: blog-writer
skill.description: Draft and add Next.js blog posts that match the existing BlogPost structure, site tone, SEO metadata, and image conventions.
skill.summary: Create SEO-ready Next.js blog posts that fit the existing BlogPost data model and content style.
skill.author: Eccentric-jamaican

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and flagged SEO wording as weak cryptography. Manual review found no executable commands, network calls, credential handling, prompt injection, or cryptographic code. The skill does intentionally guide edits to project blog files, so the remaining risk is limited to expected filesystem changes.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: False positive. The reported external command locations use Markdown inline code or a fenced content template to describe blog fields and examples. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or dynamic command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are Markdown delimiters around filenames, field names, and a prose template. There is no executable code path or instruction to run these strings as commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are SEO Text
low_findings.description: False positive. The reported weak cryptography locations discuss SEO titles, SEO descriptions, and keywords. No MD5, SHA1, cipher selection, password hashing, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is search metadata guidance, not security code. The files are Markdown instructions and contain no implementation of any weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: Expected Project File Modification
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to review blog data and add a new BlogPost object. This is legitimate for a content-writing skill, but users should review file edits before committing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly directs editing BLOG_POSTS and checking image placement. The behavior is expected, but it is still a real filesystem permission consideration.

content.user_title: Create SEO Blog Posts for Next.js
content.value_statement: Adding a blog post to a structured Next.js site can be slow and error-prone. This skill drafts posts, metadata, and placement guidance that match the existing BlogPost pattern.
actual_capabilities: Drafts blog articles that follow the site BlogPost structure.
Creates slug, date, read time, category, image, and SEO metadata guidance.
Matches the site voice for Jamaican insurance and financial planning content.
Uses Markdown headings, short paragraphs, action steps, and optional sources.
Guides placement of new posts in BLOG_POSTS and image references.
limitations: Designed for a specific Next.js blog data file and related image conventions.
Does not verify facts unless sources are provided or research is requested.
Does not create image assets by itself.
Requires human review for legal, financial, and brand accuracy.

use_cases.title: Publish Advisory Blog Posts
use_cases.description: Draft practical articles for Jamaican households about insurance, financial planning, and risk decisions.

use_cases.title: Maintain Blog Data Consistency
use_cases.description: Prepare new entries that follow existing fields, naming rules, dates, image paths, and SEO metadata.

use_cases.title: Turn Topic Ideas Into Drafts
use_cases.description: Convert a short topic brief into a structured article with headings, action steps, and a clear close.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Simple Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete first draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the blog-writer skill to draft a new post about life insurance basics for young families in Jamaica. Include title, excerpt, slug, read time, category, image suggestion, SEO fields, and article content.

prompt_templates.title: Match Existing Site Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on tone and structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing blog pattern, then create a post about emergency savings and insurance planning. Keep the tone advisory, concise, and relevant to Jamaican professionals.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Source-Based Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a blog post about underinsurance risks in Jamaica using the sources I provide. Include a numbered Sources section and keep claims tied to the supplied references.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Production-Ready Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-ready BlogPost entry for a post about retirement planning. Confirm the slug, metadata, image path, category choice, and any assumptions before editing the blog data file.

output_examples.input: Draft a post about why renters in Jamaica should understand contents insurance.
output: A clear title and excerpt focused on renters and household risk.
A suggested slug, category, read time, image path, and SEO metadata.
A Markdown article with short sections, common mistakes, action steps, and a closing invitation.

output_examples.input: Create a post from these notes about income protection for self-employed professionals.
output: A structured article that turns the notes into advisory content.
Metadata that follows the existing BlogPost conventions.
A brief assumptions list for facts, category, and image selection.

best_practices: Provide the topic, target reader, preferred category, and any source material before drafting.
Review financial and insurance claims for accuracy before publishing.
Run the site checks after adding a post to confirm rendering and metadata.
anti_patterns: Do not publish unsourced statistics or regulatory claims.
Do not add a new category when an existing category fits the post.
Do not invent image files that are not available in the project.

faq.question: What kind of blog posts does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured Next.js blog posts for insurance and financial planning topics, with metadata and SEO guidance.

faq.question: Can it edit the site files directly?
faq.answer: Yes, when used in a coding tool such as Codex or Claude Code, it can guide edits to the blog data file.

faq.question: Does it verify financial facts?
faq.answer: It can organize supplied sources, but factual claims still need human review before publication.

faq.question: Does it create images?
faq.answer: No. It suggests image paths and naming conventions, but image assets must already exist or be created separately.

faq.question: Can it work outside this Next.js site?
faq.answer: It is tailored to the referenced BlogPost structure, so other sites may need adaptation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
